subject: Sociology
title: Conflict Theory of Inequality
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Conflict Theory of Inequality.

hook.text: The rules of the game were written by the winners. Conflict theory says inequality exists because the powerful want it.

buildup.text: Rooted in Marx, conflict theory argues that the wealthy use their power to maintain their advantages.

discovery.text: Laws, schools, and media all serve ruling-class interests — often invisibly, by making inequality seem natural.

twist.text: Functionalists see consensus. Conflict theorists see domination dressed as consensus. Both have evidence.

climax.text: Conflict theory explains why inequality persists even when most people oppose it: power structures resist change.

punchline.text: Inequality persists not by accident, but by design.

quiz.question: What does conflict theory say about inequality?
options: The powerful maintain it to protect their advantages
